assertions.title: Signed-out root shows auth UI instead of the desktop shell and does not eagerly boot protected shell traffic
Guest users can reach both register and login views and each exposes a clear start action
The authenticated shell is visually distinct and exposes session-aware shell UI
Cancelled or failed passkey ceremonies stay in a retryable guest auth state
A first-time user can register a passkey and land in the authenticated shell on draft.choir-ip.com
A returning user can log in from a fresh signed-out state and land in the authenticated shell
Same-origin secure auth cookies automatically authorize documented shell bootstrap HTTP and protected WebSocket traffic
Expired access state renews through refresh rotation without a new passkey ceremony
Hard reload or a new tab at / rehydrates the authenticated shell from valid same-origin cookies
Logout revokes shell, session, HTTP, and WebSocket access across refresh and back navigation
Switching from user A to user B does not leak stale shell, proxy, or live-channel state
When refresh can no longer renew, the browser falls back cleanly to the guest auth state

summary: Ran 58 tests in Playwright suite using 1 worker. All tests passed, successfully validating all assigned assertions.
